# Pull Request

## What changes

<!-- Summary of what this PR does and why. -->

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change which adds capability)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing forks to need updates)
- [ ] Documentation only
- [ ] Refactor (no behavior change)
- [ ] Build / CI / tooling

## Checklist

- [ ] `npm run lint:md` passes
- [ ] `npm run validate` passes
- [ ] If render scripts touched: `examples/dissertation-chapter/` renders to PDF cleanly
- [ ] If schemas touched: corresponding ADR in `docs/adr/` is updated
- [ ] If user-facing behavior changed: `docs/getting-started.md` or `README.md` reflects it
- [ ] No real thesis content, secrets, or personal data committed
